title: End of Evangelion DVD Advice
wikitext:
[]: End of Evangelion DVD Advice             Quote Post by Aoren » Sat Jun 07, 2014 3:59 pm Greetings everyone. Pretty new in the forum, this is my first post. Well, I'm not that much into anime, and so, I'd never bought merchandising... but I love Evangelion and I want to have it in real life: The original EoE. I went to several comic stores and people tell me that is impossible to find it, so I went to Amazon but all the covers are different from each other. Which one of them is the true one? Also, all of them says "studio: WEA"... what is that? I couldn't find anything about studio WEA. I'm a little bit confused at this point. What should I buy? And... what about the NGE? I found the VHS but I'm concerned about their lifetime, being them VHS... then I found the Platinum version. So, platinum or VHS?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Jun 07, 2014 4:42 pm Platinum is the only decent English version of the series; it comes in several different packagings, with six or seven DVDs. I'm told that R2 version has somewhat compromised video quality compared to the R1, but I can't say that's worried me (I have the R2). EoE is long out of print. There were versions with just that film, or packaged with Death & Rebirth (which is only of interest historically, and for completists). The English-dubbed version of EoE (from Manga) has laughably poor video quality, sometimes called Vaseline-o-vision. The only (legal) way to improve on that is to buy a Japanese copy as well (or one of the recent French or Italian releases) and mux the dub stream from the English version with the video stream from one of the others. Or just get so carried away by the film that you stop noticing the quality...

[]: Re: End of Evangelion DVD Advice             Quote Post by TheDrmcvey » Sat Jun 07, 2014 4:42 pm Aoren wrote: Greetings everyone. Pretty new in the forum, this is my first post. Well, I'm not that much into anime, and so, I'd never bought merchandising... but I love Evangelion and I want to have it in real life: The original EoE. I went to several comic stores and people tell me that is impossible to find it, so I went to Amazon but all the covers are different from each other. Which one of them is the true one? Also, all of them says "studio: WEA"... what is that? I couldn't find anything about studio WEA. I'm a little bit confused at this point. What should I buy? And... what about the NGE? I found the VHS but I'm concerned about their lifetime, being them VHS... then I found the Platinum version. So, platinum or VHS?

[]:             Quote Post by Aoren » Sat Jun 07, 2014 5:36 pm Wow. Thanks for the replies. I'll check that out. Edit. How does the EoE that's currently out of prints looked like?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Jun 07, 2014 6:33 pm This is the R2 version (much cheaper than I've seen in a while), but the image is typical. The version packaged with Death & Rebirth has a completely different picture, but is sometimes cheaper for some reason. NB, while it's easy to arrange to be able to play R1 disks on many/most R2 DVD players, the opposite way about is more often not true.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jun 07, 2014 8:18 pm If you’re going to get an R2 disc, you might as well get the Japanese disc release and add some fan subs yourself. As expensive as Japanese DVDs and Blu-rays are, I really doubt you’ll find an really good out-of-print copy of the R1 release for cheaper.

[]:             Quote Post by TheDrmcvey » Sun Sep 07, 2014 12:52 pm Just bought a combo pack of D&R and EoE. Does anyone else have this in their collection and if so what did you think about the product? http://www.amazon.com/Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-Death-Rebirth/dp/B0009S4J5K/ref=sr_1_3?s=movies-tv&ie=UTF8&qid=1410112242&sr=1-3&keywords=end+of+evangelion

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Sep 07, 2014 1:34 pm TheDrmcvey wrote: Does anyone else have this in their collection and if so what did you think about the product?

[]:             Quote Post by TheDrmcvey » Sun Sep 07, 2014 1:39 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: It's basically just the two original releases in a new slipcase with two very underwhelming postcards and a mousepad, which is more like a pencil board.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Sep 07, 2014 2:11 pm The Manga version has an incorrect black level throughout, is my understanding of the issue; that certainly fits for my R2 copy.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Sep 07, 2014 3:59 pm ALL of Manga’s EoE release has terrible gamma levels. It’s just bad. I still don’t know why, since Manga’s D&R release at least had more accurate gamma settings (though the Japanese Renewal release is still the best available version thus far).

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Wed Sep 10, 2014 12:19 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: ALL of Manga’s EoE release has terrible gamma levels. It’s just bad.

[]:             Quote Post by TheDrmcvey » Wed Sep 10, 2014 12:49 pm svenge wrote: Not only that, but it's been filtered / softened terribly. The sad thing is that Manga got the exact same master as the original Japanese DVD release ( KIBA-105/6 ) but then botched it to hell and back.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Wed Sep 10, 2014 12:54 pm Man I am totally ignorant on all this picture quality stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Sep 10, 2014 1:19 pm ^ Don't lose your innocence. If you notice it once, the damage cannot be undone.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Sep 10, 2014 1:23 pm I am always envious of an opportunity to introduce someone else to EoE for the first time. It almost doesn’t matter what copy you have, the expressions of your friends faces will still be just as priceless. But just for comparison’s sake, here’s some screen caps from both Manga's DVD and the Japanese Renewal DVD. Comparison  SPOILER: Show
